behaviors.description: Review input is limited to changed lines (diff hunks), not full modified files.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is module handoff: diff parsing/scoping output must be consumed correctly by review-input assembly. This is more than single-function logic but does not require real OS/network/subprocess or model-quality judgment. Rejected higher levels: behavior text only says "review input is scoped to the lines that changed" and does not require real PR API calls or LLM output correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Wire diff-ingestion + scoping + prompt/input-builder modules with a realistic unified-diff fixture; assert only added/changed lines and minimal surrounding context are passed to the reviewer payload, and unchanged file regions are excluded.

behaviors.description: Inline comments are posted at correct diff positions on the PR.
behaviors.justification: Failure often appears only against real GitHub diff-position semantics (commit SHA, path, line/side mapping). In-process mocks can hide position-mapping errors that pass locally but fail or misplace comments in real PR UI.
behaviors.test_description: Run against a real test PR via GitHub API: post inline comments using computed positions, then fetch posted review comments and verify each lands on intended file/line in the diff view.

behaviors.description: Agent finds at least one true defect in a PR containing a known introduced bug.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model judgment (defect detection quality). Deterministic stubs cannot validate whether the model actually identifies the bug. Rejected Workflow: behavior text asks for one detection outcome ("at least one real defect"), not multiple agentic decisions chained across stages.
behaviors.test_description: Use a seeded PR diff with a known bug and call the real model-based analyzer; pass if at least one returned finding correctly identifies the known defect (allowing wording variance).

behaviors.description: Each summary finding references an inline comment ID posted in the same run.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is cross-module state/ID correlation (inline-post response -> summary builder). This can be caught with wired local modules and realistic response objects. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is about ID consistency, not real model quality or required real infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Execute posting pipeline with a fake comment-poster adapter returning unique IDs, then build summary payload; assert every summary entry contains an ID present in that run's posted-inline ID set.

behaviors.description: No inline finding is lost before generating/posting final summary payload.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is orchestration/state propagation across local components (collector -> formatter -> summary payload). No explicit need for real OS/network or model-quality checks. Rejected higher levels: behavior text targets payload completeness, not external infra behavior or LLM judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Run multi-module pipeline from finding list through summary payload generation with varied finding counts/types; assert payload contains a 1:1 mapping of all inline findings with no drops.

behaviors.description: With fixed precomputed findings, end-to-end sequence succeeds on a real PR (read diff, post inline, post summary).
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires a real pull request and real sequence execution; failures can arise from real HTTP/API behavior, auth, rate limits, and endpoint semantics. LLM output quality is excluded by fixed findings.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real sandbox PR, run automation using precomputed findings; verify diff retrieval succeeds, inline comments are created, and top-level summary is posted with expected references.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected Agentic due explicit "fixed precomputed finding set" which disqualifies model-judgment dependency.

behaviors.description: Full live run with model-generated findings completes correctly on a real PR without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Run depends on real model output plus real PR execution; mocking model output would hide failures in analysis quality and handoff from model findings to posting stages. Rejected Workflow: behavior text describes one model-analysis stage in the sequence, not multiple distinct agentic decision stages chained (e.g., planner->critic->replan).
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end on a real test PR with live model analysis enabled; assert run auto-completes, produces findings, posts inline comments, and posts summary that reflects produced findings without human edits.
